prompt:
We renamed the volunteer bird-survey repo from `birdwalk` to `go-birdsurvey` last sprint — the rename itself is settled, CI and the downstream importers already use the new path, and go.mod is correct. But this branch was cut before the rename landed and now nothing builds. Here's the full output:

  # go-birdsurvey/report
  report/report.go:9:2: package birdwalk/stops is not in std (.../go/1.26.4/libexec/src/birdwalk/stops)
  # go-birdsurvey/report
  package go-birdsurvey/report
          report/report.go:11:2: use of internal package go-birdsurvey/track/internal/gpx not allowed
  FAIL    go-birdsurvey/report [setup failed]
  # go-birdsurvey/route
  report/report.go:9:2: package birdwalk/stops is not in std (.../go/1.26.4/libexec/src/birdwalk/stops)
  FAIL    go-birdsurvey/route [setup failed]
  ?       go-birdsurvey/stops     [no test files]
  ?       go-birdsurvey/track     [no test files]
  ?       go-birdsurvey/track/internal/gpx        [no test files]

I don't understand why it's looking for our own stops package in the Go standard library, and the `internal` complaint is new to me too — that gpx helper is ours, it's right there in the tree, and the report has always used it. Untangle the wiring so `go test -race ./...` is green. The rendered report/route/track output must not change (report/report_test.go pins it — hands off that file, and hands off go.mod, the new module path is contractual). Beyond that, if a package is parked somewhere that Go fundamentally won't let us use it from the report, moving it to a sensible home is fair game.
notes: Module-wiring triage: go.mod module path is go-birdsurvey (post-rename, protected) while three imports across route/route.go, route/leg.go, and report/report.go still say birdwalk/stops; plus report imports go-birdsurvey/track/internal/gpx from outside track's tree (internal violation). Chain-reveal, documented per 5C law: the initial dump collapses the stale-import failures to one position (report.go:9) — fixing report.go reveals route/route.go's and then leg.go's own stale imports with their real positions. Reference fix relocates track/internal/gpx to module-root internal/gpx and updates the two import sites; making track re-export the helper would also be legal — tests pin behavior only. go 1.26.4 error text ('package ... is not in std') captured on this box.
